finding: The cluster-representative total order lists four keys — severity, lexicographic role, location key (§8.2), then finding text. But §8.3 clusters findings only when BOTH their location key AND class are equal, so the location key is invariant across every member of a cluster. The third comparison term can therefore never break a tie; it is a dead term. The spec's own justification ('deterministic even when two same-role findings tie on severity') is precisely the case where location key is constant and finding text is the actual decider.
suggestion: Drop the location-key term: the §8.4 order is severity → role → finding text. If a finer tiebreak before finding text is genuinely wanted, use the full `location` string (which can differ within a cluster), not the reduced location key — the reduced key is provably constant per cluster and consumes no decision.
